svg.text: PANEL 2 · INVOCATION LIFECYCLE (operator says "run the debate team on X")

g.text: G2 native task deps for pipeline

svg.text: PANEL 5 · MENTAL MODEL — THE HIERARCHY IS ALREADY THERE (§3.4)

g.text: = main Claude session (= team-lead in code)
NOT a thin task-router · synthesizes outputs
Pure doc framing. No new code, no new role file, no new architectural layer. The PO ↔ tech-lead handoff is where your intent ends and Claude's autonomy begins —
the moment you say "run the X team on Y" and the lead takes over until it hands you back a summary.md. "Team lead" is the implementation term, "tech lead" the mental model.

svg.text: PANEL 6 · HOW TO INVOKE A TEAM (getting the lead to follow PLAYBOOK.md)
Claude Code itself does not auto-load PLAYBOOK.md when you say "run the X team". One of the three options below must be in place, or the lead will improvise.

g.text: works today · project-scoped · zero friction at invoke
When I say "run the X team on Y",
"run the improvement team on regime-config bugs"
pro: natural language · scoped to project
works today · zero setup · friction every invoke
and run the improvement team on regime-config
pro: no setup · con: friction · easy to forget the prefix
not built yet · roadmap phase C · one-line invoke
pro: terse · cross-project · cons: not yet built

svg.text: solid = sync · dashed = retry/response · yellow = patch / variant / recommended · circle = parallel worker
